name: OES: Adaptability Work Ready Learner Badge
description:
To earn the Oregon Employability Skills, Work Ready Learner Badge, students refine their vocabulary and deepen their understanding of how employability skills are applied both in the classroom and the workplace. The focus is on helping learners see concrete connections between these skills and real-world applications, preparing them to take the next step toward full work readiness.
While not yet at the level of demonstrating complete proficiency, students earning the Learner Badge show an increasing ability to recognize and articulate how skills learned in academic settings translate into workplace traits and responsibilities.

Learners can earn the Learner Badge by completing the following steps:
-Module Completion: Engage with more advanced instructional modules that emphasize the practical application of OES skills. Students understand and can articulate a common language that encourages them to connect OES skills to preparation to be work-ready in the future. 
-Understanding and Application: Learners begin to apply their growing skill set by sharing personal stories and experiences, exploring how these skills are used both in the classroom and in potential workplace environments. This helps bridge the gap between understanding and application, fostering readiness for more advanced skill demonstration.
-Proficiency Preparation: Learners complete assessments and reflective activities that further solidify their knowledge. While they are not yet required to demonstrate full proficiency, they must show readiness to understand and apply these skills in increasingly complex situations, preparing them for the top-level work-ready academic badge.
-Social Emotional Connection: Recognizing and applying these skills enhances emotional regulation and social awareness, empowering students to effectively navigate challenges and transitions. By embracing a growth mindset, students learn to adapt strategies, collaborate with others, and contribute to shared success in both academic and real-world environments. These abilities prepare them for future endeavors where flexibility and teamwork are essential.

criteria.narrative:
To earn the Oregon Employability Skills, Work Readiness Adaptability Learner Badge, students have demonstrated foundational knowledge and a working knowledge of the badged skill through the Oregon Employability Skills program. This badge is awarded by completing modular instruction, participating in work-related activities, and achieving a minimum score of 70% on the assessment. 

The curriculum teaches adaptability through the following core traits:
1. Sees when things change and recognizes that change can be an opportunity.
2. Open to new experiences, including work environments, roles, and tasks.
3. Acknowledges the viewpoints of others and accepts suggestions to get the job done.
4. Handles average amounts of stress, positively uses feedback, and learns from things that go wrong.

Students learn about these core traits by watching a video that showcases how an Oregon company implements them in the workplace. Following the video, students engage in an aligned activity to reinforce their understanding and application of these traits.

Students are evaluated on these criteria through both the quiz and the "Tell Your Story" sections of the assessment. This is an auto graded assessment with a 70% required for badging.

criteria.narrative:
To earn the Oregon Employability Skills, Work Readiness Analysis Solution Mindset Learner Badge, students have demonstrated foundational knowledge and a working knowledge of the badged skill through the Oregon Employability Skills program. This badge is awarded by completing modular instruction, participating in work-related activities, and achieving a minimum score of 70% on the assessment. 

The curriculum teaches analysis solution mindset through the following core traits:
1. Considers different points of view; tries to understand why information is presented the way it is.
2. Sees problems and needs in society, the community, or the workplace. Looks at the bigger picture when finding a way to solve a problem.
3. Sees themes or patterns in data and connects information from different subject areas. Uses critical thinking skills.
4. Considers different ways of solving problems; tests ideas to find the one that works best.

Students learn about these core traits by watching a video that showcases how an Oregon company implements them in the workplace. Following the video, students engage in an aligned activity to reinforce their understanding and application of these traits.

Students are evaluated on these criteria through both the quiz and the "Tell Your Story" sections of the assessment. This is an auto graded assessment with a 70% required for badging.

criteria.narrative:
To earn the Oregon Employability Skills, Work Readiness Communication Learner Badge, students have demonstrated foundational knowledge and a working knowledge of the badged skill through the Oregon Employability Skills program. This badge is awarded by completing modular instruction, participating in work-related activities, and achieving a minimum score of 70% on the assessment. 

The curriculum teaches communication through the following core traits:
1, Speaks in a way that is appropriate for the workplace and that others will understand, and writes with correct grammar and rules.
2. Chooses words with care, uses nonverbal methods to clarify meaning, and uses common social rules for interacting with others. Knows how to communicate across roles.
3. Knows when and how to communicate with email, the internet, digital communication and social media, and other computer applications.
4. Is an active listener - listens with empathy, paraphrases for understanding, and asks questions for clarification before actively communicating to avoid misunderstanding.

Students learn about these core traits by watching a video that showcases how an Oregon company implements them in the workplace. Following the video, students engage in an aligned activity to reinforce their understanding and application of these traits.

This badge is awarded by completing modular instruction, participating in work-related activities, and achieving a minimum score of 70% on the assessment. 

The curriculum teaches resilience through the following core traits:
1. Sets priorities and goals. Sees possible outcomes to actions and creates backup plans.
2. Bounces back when things go wrong by figuring out what happened and how to learn from it.
3. Takes feedback and deals directly with conflict positively, and can listen to others and speak up for themselves.
4. Knows that personal growth and skill development is an essential part of life.

Students learn about these core traits by watching a video that showcases how an Oregon company implements them in the workplace. Following the video, students engage in an aligned activity to reinforce their understanding and application of these traits.

This badge is awarded by completing modular instruction, participating in work-related activities, and achieving a minimum score of 70% on the assessment. 

The curriculum teaches self-awareness through the following core traits:
1. Is realistic about personal strengths, skills, and areas of needed growth.
2. Looks for jobs that are a good match for personal strengths and skills.
3. Acknowledges personal responsibility in actions and words; seeks to control emotions and behavior even under stress; tries to do the right thing.
4. Exhibits work-appropriate behavior, including showing up on time and applies guidelines or rules of the work setting to stay safe.

Students learn about these core traits by watching a video that showcases how an Oregon company implements them in the workplace. Following the video, students engage in an aligned activity to reinforce their understanding and application of these traits.

This badge is awarded by completing modular instruction, participating in work-related activities, and achieving a minimum score of 70% on the assessment. 

The curriculum teaches social diversity through the following core traits:
1. Recognizes and values internal diversity, appreciating unique qualities and contributions regardless of origin, appearance, or beliefs.
2 Shares unique backgrounds, experiences, and cultural knowledge, understanding how they shape external diversity.
3. Understands how differences in roles, experiences, company size, mission, and values contribute to workplace diversity.
4. Advocates for diversity by asking thoughtful questions and promoting respectful, inclusive behaviors to model diversity.
